stories.headline: SK Hynix ADRs Surge 14% After Record $26.5 Billion US Offering
stories.summary: SK Hynix Inc.’s American depositary receipts surged 14% above their offering price, after the South Korean memory chipmaker raised $26.5 billion in the largest-ever US listing by a foreign company.

stories.headline: SK Hynix Open to Issuing More US Shares, SK Group Chairman Says
stories.summary: SK Hynix Inc. is open to issuing more US shares should returns prove strong and the company’s stock price remains stable, SK Group Chairman Chey Tae-won said.

stories.headline: EasyJet Gets £5.7 Billion Apollo Offer in Possible Bidding War
stories.summary: EasyJet Plc received an offer from private equity firm Apollo Global Management for 715 pence a share that beats a rival proposal from Castlelake LP, a surprise twist in the takeover saga that sets up a possible bidding war between the two US investment funds.

stories.headline: Europeans Are Changing Saving Habits to Invest More, ING Says

stories.headline: SK Hynix Set to Make Record-Setting US Debut
stories.summary: SK Hynix Inc. raised $26.5 billion in its American depositary share offering, the largest ever US first-time share sale by a foreign company. Bloomberg's Ed Ludlow reports.  (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Trump Says He Won’t Sign Housing Bill, in Protest Over Stalled Voting Measure
stories.summary: The president did not say he would veto the housing legislation, so it would still become law unless he does. But his pronouncement reflects a growing rift between him and Senate Republicans.

stories.headline: Trump Administration Fires Members of Independent Election Group
stories.summary: The firings and a resignation render the Election Assistance Commission useless. The moves come as President Trump seeks to impose control over how ballots will be counted in the midterms.

stories.headline: Bombing Iran Didn’t Work for Trump. Neither Did a Tentative Cease-Fire. Is There a Plan C?

stories.headline: French economy minister on Spain's debt proposal: 'It's interesting'
stories.summary: French Economy Minister Roland Lescure reacts to Spain's proposal for a new joint EU debt instrument worth up to €850 billion, while warning of potential risks.
